file.content:
name: ci
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
  workflow_dispatch:
env:
  CACHIX_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CACHIX_AUTH_TOKEN }}
  CARGO_INCREMENTAL: 0
  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true
permissions:
  contents: read
jobs:
  ci:
    name: ${{ matrix.os }}
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest]
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      - uses: actions-rs/toolchain@v1
        if: ${{ matrix.os == 'windows-latest' }}
        with:
          components: clippy
          profile: minimal
          toolchain: stable
      - uses: actions-rs/toolchain@v1
        if: ${{ matrix.os == 'windows-latest' }}
        with:
          components: rustfmt
          profile: minimal
          toolchain: nightly
      - uses: cachix/install-nix-action@v31
        if: ${{ matrix.os != 'windows-latest' }}
        with:
          nix_path: nixpkgs=channel:nixos-unstable
      - uses: cachix/cachix-action@v16
        if: ${{ matrix.os != 'windows-latest' && env.CACHIX_AUTH_TOKEN != '' }}
        with:
          authToken: ${{ env.CACHIX_AUTH_TOKEN }}
          name: zoxide
      - name: Setup cache
        uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2.8.2
        with:
          key: ${{ matrix.os }}
      - name: Install just
        uses: taiki-e/install-action@v2
        with:
          tool: just
      - name: Run lints + tests
        run: just lint test

content.thinking:
Perfect! Now I can see the workflow. The key steps for ubuntu-latest are:
1. actions/checkout@v6 (with fetch-depth: 0)
2. cachix/install-nix-action@v31 (nix_path: nixpkgs=channel:nixos-unstable)
3. cachix/cachix-action@v16 (for caching)
4. Setup cache (Swatinem/rust-cache@v2.8.2)
5. Install just (using taiki-e/install-action@v2)
6. Run: `just lint test`

So the job:
- Uses Ubuntu latest
- Installs Nix
- Uses Nix to set up the environment (via shell.nix)
- Installs `just` task runner
- Runs `just lint test`

let
  pkgs = import (builtins.fetchTarball
    "https://github.com/NixOS/nixpkgs/archive/ec9ef366451af88284d7dfd18ee017b7e86a0710.tar.gz") {
      overlays = [ rust ];
    };
  rust = import (builtins.fetchTarball
    "https://github.com/oxalica/rust-overlay/archive/026e8fedefd6b167d92ed04b195c658d95ffc7a5.tar.gz");

  rust-nightly =
    pkgs.rust-bin.selectLatestNightlyWith (toolchain: toolchain.minimal);
  cargo-udeps = pkgs.writeShellScriptBin "cargo-udeps" ''
    export RUSTC="${rust-nightly}/bin/rustc";
    export CARGO="${rust-nightly}/bin/cargo";
    exec "${pkgs.cargo-udeps}/bin/cargo-udeps" "$@"
  '';
in pkgs.mkShell {
  buildInputs = [
    # Rust
    (pkgs.rust-bin.selectLatestNightlyWith (toolchain: toolchain.rustfmt))
    pkgs.rust-bin.stable.latest.default

    # Shells
    pkgs.bash
    pkgs.dash
    pkgs.elvish
    pkgs.fish
    pkgs.ksh
    pkgs.nushell
    pkgs.powershell
    pkgs.tcsh
    pkgs.xonsh
    pkgs.zsh

    # Tools
    cargo-udeps
    pkgs.cargo-msrv
    pkgs.cargo-nextest
    pkgs.cargo-udeps
    pkgs.just
    pkgs.mandoc
    pkgs.nixfmt
    pkgs.nodePackages.markdownlint-cli
    pkgs.python3Packages.black
    pkgs.python3Packages.mypy
    pkgs.python3Packages.pylint
    pkgs.shellcheck
    pkgs.shfmt
    pkgs.yamlfmt

    # Dependencies
    pkgs.cacert
    pkgs.fzf
    pkgs.git
    pkgs.libiconv
  ];

  CARGO_TARGET_DIR = "target_nix";
}

All of these are wrapped with `nix-shell --cores 0 --pure --run ...`

The problem is, setting up Nix in a Docker container can be complex. However, since the shell.nix defines what dependencies are needed, I can:

Option 2: Manually install all the tools from shell.nix into a regular ubuntu container

Option 1 might be cleaner but could be slower. Option 2 gives me more control.

- It uses cachix/cachix-action to cache

For a Docker reproduction, I'll try to use a Nix-based approach first since that's what the actual CI does. I can use a nixos/nix:latest base image or an ubuntu image with Nix installed.

input.content:
FROM ubuntu:22.04

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# Install system dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    curl \
    git \
    xz-utils \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install Nix
RUN curl -L https://nixos.org/nix/install | sh -s -- --no-daemon && \
    mkdir -p /etc/nix && \
    echo "system-features = nixos-test" > /etc/nix/nix.conf

# Source Nix environment
ENV PATH=/root/.nix-profile/bin:/root/.nix-profile/sbin:$PATH

WORKDIR /app
COPY repo/ /app/

# Copy and run the test script
COPY run.sh /run.sh
RUN chmod +x /run.sh

ENTRYPOINT ["/run.sh"]

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -e

# Source Nix environment
source /root/.nix-profile/etc/profile.d/nix.sh

# Run lints and tests using nix-shell
cd /app
nix-shell --cores 0 --pure --run 'just lint test' || {
    echo "FINAL_STATUS = FAIL"
    exit 1
}

echo "FINAL_STATUS = SUCCESS"

input.content:
FROM ubuntu:22.04

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# Install system dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    curl \
    git \
    xz-utils \
    build-essential \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install Nix - create /nix directory first
RUN mkdir -m 0755 /nix && chown root /nix
RUN curl -L https://nixos.org/nix/install | sh -s -- --no-daemon && \
    mkdir -p /etc/nix && \
    echo "system-features = nixos-test" > /etc/nix/nix.conf

# Source Nix environment
ENV PATH=/root/.nix-profile/bin:/root/.nix-profile/sbin:$PATH

WORKDIR /app
COPY repo/ /app/

# Copy and run the test script
COPY run.sh /run.sh
RUN chmod +x /run.sh

ENTRYPOINT ["/run.sh"]

FROM ubuntu:22.04

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# Install system dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    curl \
    git \
    xz-utils \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install Nix
RUN curl -L https://nixos.org/nix/install | sh -s -- --no-daemon && \
    mkdir -p /etc/nix && \
    echo "system-features = nixos-test" > /etc/nix/nix.conf

# Source Nix environment
ENV PATH=/root/.nix-profile/bin:/root/.nix-profile/sbin:$PATH

WORKDIR /app
COPY repo/ /app/

# Copy and run the test script
COPY run.sh /run.sh
RUN chmod +x /run.sh

ENTRYPOINT ["/run.sh"]

content.thinking:
The Nix installer is looking for a nixbld group which is typically created during multi-user installation. Since we're in a container, let me try a different approach - I'll use a Nix-based base image or manually set up the required groups. Let me try using the flydra/nixos-linux base image instead, which is a minimal NixOS image.

Actually, another approach would be to try setting up Nix without the build-users-group, which is more suitable for single-user installations in containers. Let me modify the Dockerfile to work around this.

input.content:
FROM ubuntu:22.04

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# Install system dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    curl \
    git \
    xz-utils \
    build-essential \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install Nix - create /nix directory and disable build-users-group for single-user install
RUN mkdir -m 0755 /nix && chown root /nix
RUN curl -L https://nixos.org/nix/install | bash -s -- --no-daemon && \
    mkdir -p /etc/nix && \
    echo "build-users-group =" >> /etc/nix/nix.conf

# Source Nix environment for subsequent RUN commands
ENV PATH=/root/.nix-profile/bin:/root/.nix-profile/sbin:$PATH

WORKDIR /app
COPY repo/ /app/

# Copy and run the test script
COPY run.sh /run.sh
RUN chmod +x /run.sh

ENTRYPOINT ["/run.sh"]

    curl \

    build-essential \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

RUN mkdir -m 0755 /nix && chown root /nix

    echo "system-features = nixos-test" > /etc/nix/nix.conf

WORKDIR /app

input.content:
FROM ubuntu:22.04

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# Install system dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    curl \
    git \
    xz-utils \
    build-essential \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Create nixbld group and users for Nix
RUN groupadd -g 30000 nixbld && \
    for i in {1..10}; do useradd -c "Nix build user $i" -d /var/empty -g nixbld -G nixbld -M -N -r -s /sbin/nologin nixbld$i; done

# Install Nix
RUN mkdir -m 0755 /nix && chown root /nix && \
    curl -L https://nixos.org/nix/install | bash -s -- --no-daemon && \
    mkdir -p /etc/nix && \
    echo "build-users-group = nixbld" > /etc/nix/nix.conf && \
    echo "sandbox = false" >> /etc/nix/nix.conf

# Source Nix environment for subsequent RUN commands
ENV PATH=/root/.nix-profile/bin:/root/.nix-profile/sbin:$PATH

WORKDIR /app
COPY repo/ /app/

# Copy and run the test script
COPY run.sh /run.sh
RUN chmod +x /run.sh

ENTRYPOINT ["/run.sh"]

FROM ubuntu:22.04

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    curl \
    git \
    xz-utils \
    build-essential \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install Nix - create /nix directory and disable build-users-group for single-user install
RUN mkdir -m 0755 /nix && chown root /nix
RUN curl -L https://nixos.org/nix/install | bash -s -- --no-daemon && \
    mkdir -p /etc/nix && \
    echo "build-users-group =" >> /etc/nix/nix.conf

content.thinking:
Ah, the issue is that `nix-shell` is not found because we need to ensure the Nix environment is properly sourced before running the commands. The ENV PATH setting doesn't apply within the nix-shell scripts since it's running as a subprocess.

Let me modify the run.sh script to properly source the Nix environment before running nix-shell.

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -e

# Source Nix environment
source /root/.nix-profile/etc/profile.d/nix.sh

# Run lints and tests using nix-shell
cd /app

# Export the nix environment variables for subshells
export NIX_PATH=nixpkgs=channel:nixos-unstable

# Run the lints and tests
if nix-shell --cores 0 --pure --run 'just lint test'; then
    echo "FINAL_STATUS = SUCCESS"
else
    echo "FINAL_STATUS = FAIL"
    exit 1
fi

tool_use_result.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -e

# Source Nix environment
source /root/.nix-profile/etc/profile.d/nix.sh

# Run lints and tests using nix-shell
cd /app
nix-shell --cores 0 --pure --run 'just lint test' || {
    echo "FINAL_STATUS = FAIL"
    exit 1
}

echo "FINAL_STATUS = SUCCESS"

content.thinking:
The issue is still the same - `nix-shell` is not found when running inside the nix-shell environment. The problem is that when we run `nix-shell --pure`, it creates a new shell with a minimal PATH, and `nix-shell` itself is not available inside that pure shell.

Let me reconsider the approach. Looking at the justfile again, it runs `nix-shell --cores 0 --pure --run 'cargo-fmt --all --check'`, which means we need to run nix-shell commands. But our run.sh is already trying to run nix-shell.

The issue is that when nix-shell starts with --pure, it strips the PATH and other environment variables, and nix-shell itself is not available inside that environment. This is by design in pure shells.

Let me try a different approach: instead of running `nix-shell --pure --run 'just lint test'`, I should run each command directly inside the nix-shell. Or I should modify the justfile recipes to not use nix-shell recursively.

Actually, looking more carefully at the justfile, each recipe uses `nix-shell --cores 0 --pure --run '<command>'`. This means each line in the justfile starts its own nix-shell. So when I run `nix-shell --pure --run 'just lint test'`, the justfile's nix-shell commands won't work because --pure removes access to nix-shell.

Let me change the approach: I'll remove the --pure flag when calling nix-shell from run.sh. This way, the environment will be available inside the shell for the nested nix-shell commands.

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -e

# Source Nix environment
source /root/.nix-profile/etc/profile.d/nix.sh

# Run lints and tests using nix-shell
cd /app

# Export the nix environment variables for subshells
export NIX_PATH=nixpkgs=channel:nixos-unstable

# Run the lints and tests without --pure so that nix-shell is available for the justfile recipes
if nix-shell --cores 0 --run 'just lint test'; then
    echo "FINAL_STATUS = SUCCESS"
else
    echo "FINAL_STATUS = FAIL"
    exit 1
fi

fi
